name: Ao Bing
shortDescription: Ao Bing was a dragon prince in Chinese mythology, known for his arrogance and conflict with the deity Nezha.
description: Ao Bing was the third son of Ao Guang, the Dragon King of the Eastern Sea in Chinese mythology. He is best known for his antagonistic role in the legends surrounding Nezha, a powerful deity. Ao Bing, arrogant and spoiled due to his status, often abused his power, causing trouble for the local people. He demanded tributes and generally acted without regard for justice or compassion. His most infamous act was his confrontation with Nezha, who was bathing in the sea. Ao Bing, angered by Nezha's actions (which were, according to some versions, inadvertently harming his underwater palace), attacked the boy. Nezha, possessing divine powers and weapons, ultimately defeated and killed Ao Bing. This act of slaying a dragon prince had severe consequences, leading to further conflict between Nezha's family and the Dragon Kings. Ao Guang, enraged by the death of his son, sought revenge and demanded Nezha's life as compensation. The conflict escalated, involving celestial intervention and ultimately leading to Nezha's self-sacrifice and subsequent rebirth as a powerful deity. Ao Bing's story serves as a cautionary tale about the dangers of unchecked power and arrogance, contrasting with the righteous and often rebellious spirit of Nezha. The tale highlights the importance of balance and justice in the cosmos and the consequences of disrupting the natural order. Ao Bing's existence is intrinsically linked to the Dragon Kings' role in controlling the seas and rainfall, reflecting the importance of water and weather in Chinese culture and mythology.

sources.title: Journey to the West (Xi You Ji)
sources.author: Wu Cheng'en

sources.title: Dragons in Chinese Art and Culture
sources.author: Filipiak, K.
